name: ETH Near-Miss Monitoring Design
scope: Design-only checkpoint for future ETH shadow/no-order monitoring. No runtime implementation, PAPER deployment, LIVE deployment, threshold change, or production DB change.
edge_rationale: ETH trial-00095 transfer and BTC+ETH portfolio replay are decision-grade offline evidence, but ETH requires symbol-specific live shadow diagnostics before any PAPER orders. The design defines how to observe ETH sweep-depth near-misses without contaminating BTC M4 conclusions.
counterparty_or_market_mechanism: No new trading mechanism is tested. Monitoring observes whether ETH live sweep/reclaim geometry and threshold proximity resemble the audited offline transfer behavior.
required_data: BTCUSDT M4 data kept separate for comparison only
timeframe: 15m decision cycle, future shadow_no_orders runtime mode
baseline_reference: ETH_TRIAL_00095_TRANSFER_FEASIBILITY_V1 and BTC PAPER M4 near-miss monitoring

variables.min_sweep_depth_pct: frozen at trial-00095 unless later audited stability milestone changes it

frozen_assumptions: No ETH PAPER orders during shadow_no_orders mode.
No ETH threshold change from shadow monitoring alone.
BTC M4 remains independent and must not aggregate ETH near-miss data.
Nested near_miss_diagnostics.sweep_depth_pct is mandatory for near-miss records.
ETH PAPER requires a later audited shadow checkpoint and user approval.
expected_observation: A useful future ETH shadow phase records symbol-explicit sweep-depth, blocker, near-miss, governance, and portfolio diagnostics while placing zero ETH orders.
acceptance_criteria: Design clearly blocks runtime changes and ETH PAPER approval.
Design defines ETH-specific payload fields and near-miss buckets.
Design defines Day 3, Day 14, and Day 30 shadow checkpoints.
Design separates ETH shadow conclusions from BTC M4 conclusions.
Design requires separate threshold stability research before ETH threshold changes.
kill_criteria: Any runtime implementation is included in this milestone.
Any ETH PAPER approval is implied.
Any ETH threshold lowering is authorized directly from monitoring design.
BTC and ETH near-miss data are combined into one threshold conclusion.
failure_modes: Shadow payload shape diverges from BTC M4 corrected contract.
ETH shadow diagnostics become a hidden trading approval path.
ETH monitoring contaminates BTC M4 interpretation.
Threshold changes are inferred from live near-misses without offline ceteris-paribus replay.
